title: Hosdurg Fort
tagline: Hosdurg Fort — heritage city of Kerala

overview.description: Hosdurg Fort or Hosadurga Fort (transl. New fort), also known as Kanhangad Fort, is a fort in Kanhangad in Kasaragod district of Kerala, India. Somashekara Nayaka of the Keladi Nayaka dynasty of Ikkeri built the fort. The place is made well known by the Nithyanandasram with 45 caves. To the south-west of the fort is a temple that was also built by the Nayaks.

topPlaces.name: Hosdurg taluk
topPlaces.description: Hosdurg is one of four taluks that constitute the Kasaragod District, Kerala. Others are Kasaragod, Manjeshwaram and Vellarikundu taluks.

topPlaces.name: Thayannur, Kasaragod
topPlaces.description: Thayannur is a small village situated in the Kasaragod district of Kerala in India. It is located on the way between Kanhangad and Kalichanadukkam.

topPlaces.name: Pullur, Kasaragod
topPlaces.description: Pullur is a small town located in the Hosdurg taluk of Kasaragod district, Kerala. It is situated 10 km from the sub-district headquarters, Kanhangad and 30 km from the district headquarters, Kasaragod.

topPlaces.name: Ambalathara, Kasaragod
topPlaces.description: Ambalathara is a village in Kasaragod district in the state of Kerala, India.

topPlaces.name: Athinhal
topPlaces.description: Athinhal is a coastal village in Kasaragod district in the Indian state of Kerala. It is 28 km from the district capital of Kasaragod and one kilometer north of Kanhangad.

topPlaces.name: Aingoth
topPlaces.description: Aingoth is a settlement in Kerala, India. It is located on the NH17 between Nileshwar and Kanhangad towns on the Cochin-Mangalore National Highway in Kasaragod District, Kanhangad Municipality.

topPlaces.name: Kolavayal
topPlaces.description: Kolavayal is a place in Ajanur village in Kanhangad town, Kasargod district, Kerala, India. It is a good place.

topPlaces.name: Ajanur
topPlaces.description: Ajanur (also anglicized Ajanoor) is a grama panchayat and also a village of the same name in Kasaragod district of Kerala, India. It is the fifth largest town in Kasaragod district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Hosdurg Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Hosdurg Fort is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Hosdurg Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Mangaluru International Airport (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Mangaluru Central (68 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Hosdurg Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Hosdurg Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Hosdurg Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Hosdurg Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Hosdurg Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Hosdurg taluk, Thayannur, Kasaragod, Pullur, Kasaragod, Ambalathara, Kasaragod, Athinhal.
